_meta.purpose: Lexicon for the grounding gate: detect a build-behavior DIAGNOSTIC question about the current project (why does X render/break, where is Y, what's happening with Z) so the agent is forced to read the code + beats BEFORE probing the running app. Consumed by grounding-gate.sh (UserPromptSubmit, arms + injects) and indirectly by grounding-guard.sh (PreToolUse, denies browser/screenshot probes until grounding happened).
_meta.fire_rule: Arm when (a DIAGNOSTIC_FRAME or a BROKEN_STATE phrase) co-occurs with (a BEHAVIOR_VERB or a BUILD_NOUN). Informational framings (what is X / how to use X / explain X / define X) are suppressed, same as the sidecoach hook. Respect the cooldown after a disarm so an active debugging session is not re-nagged.
_meta.why: 2026-06-05: the agent answered 'why aren't my changes showing in the bottom-left panel' by screenshotting/clicking the running app instead of grepping justify/core/changes-panel.ts (which hardcodes bottom:68px;left:20px) and reading the panel beats. The answer was one grep away. This moves the failed CLAUDE.md rule into a mechanical gate (decision_hook_layer_as_enforcement).
_meta.pattern_syntax: Regex fragments, matched case-insensitively against a sanitized prompt (code fences/URLs stripped) with the same hyphen-aware boundaries the sidecoach hook uses. Tune freely - this file is the single source of truth for grounding-gate triggering.

nudge: This reads as a build-behavior diagnostic question about THIS project. Before you probe the running app (screenshots, clicks, DevTools), GROUND in the source of truth: grep the relevant code path and read the named beats from MEMORY.md. The running app is for VERIFYING an understanding, not forming one - the answer to how the build behaves lives in the code and the beats, usually one grep away. Probing tools (browser/screenshot) are gated until you have read code or beats this turn.
